name: Sainte-Croix-du-Mont, Verdelais et Saint-Macaire - loop in Entre-deux-Mers
name_original: Sainte-Croix-du-Mont, Verdelais et Saint-Macaire - boucle dans l'Entre-deux-Mers
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Saint-Macaire traverse a landscape characterized by the Garonne River, the extensive Landes forest, and the undulating countryside of Entre-Deux-Mers. The region features well-maintained pathways along the Garonne Lateral Canal and former railway lines like the Roger Lapébie Cycle Path, offering varied terrain from flat, shaded routes to gentle ascents through vineyards. This area provides a mix of waterside paths, woodlands, and open agricultural land, with elevations generally remaining below 200 meters. Best touring cycling routes around Saint-Macaire The most popular touring cycling route is Sainte-Croix-du-Mont, Verdelais et Saint-Macaire - loop in Entre-deux-Mers, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This route explores the Entre-Deux-Mers region, connecting historic towns and scenic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Canal latéral à la Garonne – Fontet Marina loop from Saint-Macaire, a moderate 36.9 miles (59.4 km) path. This route follows the Garonne Lateral Canal, offering flat, shaded sections and views of the waterway. Local touring cyclists also love the Canal latéral à la Garonne – Church in Langon loop from Saint-Macaire, a 66.1 miles (106.4 km) trail leading through the Garonne Lateral Canal area, often completed in about 6 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Saint-Macaire is defined by routes along the Garonne River, through the Entre-Deux-Mers countryside, and beside the Garonne Lateral Canal. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat, easy rides to more challenging routes with moderate elevation changes. The routes in Saint-Macaire are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Macaire's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Saint-Macaire?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Saint-Macaire, offering diverse experiences for various skill levels. These routes traverse landscapes characterized by the Garonne River, the Landes forest, and the undulating countryside of Entre-Deux-Mers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Saint-Macaire?
A: The terrain around Saint-Macaire is varied. You'll find well-maintained, almost flat and shaded pathways along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2605415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal&lt;/a&gt; and former railway lines like the Roger Lapébie Cycle Path. Other routes offer gentle ascents through vineyards and open agricultural land, with elevations generally remaining below 200 meters.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for families in Saint-Macaire?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for families. The Roger Lapébie Cycle Path, built on an old railway line, is particularly suitable as it's almost flat and well-shaded, providing a safe and scenic ride through woodlands, meadows, and vineyards.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Saint-Macaire area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961514357/sainte-croix-du-mont-verdelais-et-saint-macaire-loop-in-entre-deux-mers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sainte-Croix-du-Mont, Verdelais et Saint-Macaire - loop in Entre-deux-Mers&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.2-mile (30.9 km) route that explores historic towns and offers scenic views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see while cycling around Saint-Macaire?
A: While cycling, you can explore the historic town of Saint-Macaire itself, with its medieval church and noble buildings. Routes often pass by impressive chateaux in the Premier Crux Sauterne region, such as Budos and Villandraut. You can also visit charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5191084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sainte-Croix-du-Mont&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views of the Garonne valley, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5191261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdelais&lt;/a&gt; with its basilica and Cussol mill. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2605415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal&lt;/a&gt; also features interesting locks, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3968509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castets Lock, no. 53&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Saint-Macaire?
A: Yes, Saint-Macaire offers 6 easy touring cycling routes. These often follow the flat, shaded paths along the Garonne Lateral Canal, providing a comfortable introduction to cycling in the region. These routes are perfect for those looking for a relaxed ride without significant elevation changes.

Q: What about more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult touring cycling routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38583767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal latéral à la Garonne – Church in Langon loop from Saint-Macaire&lt;/a&gt;, a 66.1-mile (106.4 km) trail that takes about 6 hours 16 minutes to complete, offering a longer and more demanding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Saint-Macaire?
A: The region is enjoyable for cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially through the vineyards. Summer can be warm, but many routes, particularly those along the Garonne Lateral Canal and the Roger Lapébie Cycle Path, are well-shaded, providing relief from the sun.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes in Saint-Macaire?
A: Yes, Saint-Macaire and the surrounding towns are generally well-equipped for visitors. You can typically find parking in the town centers or near popular trailheads, especially those accessing the Garonne Lateral Canal or the Roger Lapébie Cycle Path. Specific parking availability may vary by route starting point.

Q: Can I explore vineyards and chateaux by bike in this region?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its wine, and many cycling routes pass directly through the countryside of Premier Crux Sauterne. You can pedal past impressive chateaux like Budos, Yquem, Clos Haut Peyraguey, Villandraut, and Rayne Vignau, offering a unique way to experience the area's viticultural heritage.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes typically take around Saint-Macaire?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961514357/sainte-croix-du-mont-verdelais-et-saint-macaire-loop-in-entre-deux-mers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sainte-Croix-du-Mont, Verdelais et Saint-Macaire - loop in Entre-deux-Mers&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours. Longer, more difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38583767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal latéral à la Garonne – Church in Langon loop from Saint-Macaire&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to over 6 hours.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Saint-Macaire?
A: The touring cycling routes around Saint-Macaire are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views along the Garonne River and Canal, and the opportunity to explore historic towns and vineyards.
tips:
Sainte-Croix-du-Mont is built on a rocky outcrop overlooking the Garonne valley and the Sauternes vineyards. At the foot of the imposing castle of Tastes, there is an interpretive trail and a belvedere from which you can see the Pyrenees on a clear day.

The town also has a unique geological site in France: a cave made up of oyster fossils dating back 22 million years. Have !

On the way to Santiago de Compostela, Verdelais has a large basilica, an impressive Calvary (in the cemetery where Toulouse-Lautrec is buried), as well as a mill still in operation: the mill of Cussol. The central alley of the village, lined with plane trees is ideal for taking a break in the shade!
View of the town of Saint-Macaire and its ramparts. From Chemin du Port, along the Garonne. Ideal for a lunch break!

waypoints.name: Chemin du Port - View of Saint-Macaire
